Punta Gorda_FL.script: Nobody talks about this pre-construction strategy in Punta Gorda, and it's the reason some buyers are locking in equity before the foundation is even poured. Right now there are new builds in Babcock Ranch and the Burnt Store corridor starting in the low $300s. You buy at pre-construction pricing, a local tenant covers your mortgage while you wait for closing, and by the time you get your keys the home has already appreciated. I've watched buyers walk into $40,000 to $60,000 in equity just from the build timeline. Babcock Ranch specifically has a waitlist for rentals right now because demand is outpacing supply. And Punta Gorda's median sale price climbed 11% last year. This isn't theory. This is happening right now. Are you looking to buy this year or are you still in research mode? Tell me below and I'll send you the exact pre-construction projects I'd look at in your price range.

Punta Gorda_FL.title: Tristan Ahumada | Foreclosure filings jumped 20% year-over ...
Punta Gorda_FL.caption:
Foreclosure filings jumped 20% year-over-year in February, marking a full year of steady increases. While national levels remain below historic norms, several states are seeing a significant rise in activity.

The top 3 states for foreclosures right now:

1. Indiana (Highest rate in the nation)
2. South Carolina
3. Florida (Holding the #3 spot for two consecutive months)

In states like Florida, a combination of rising insurance costs, high pandemic-era home prices, and increasing condo fees are making it more difficult for homeowners to keep up.

Are you noticing more “For Sale” signs in your area? Let us know which state you’re in in the comments.

#RealEstate #HousingMarket
Punta Gorda_FL.script: Florida just hit the number 3 spot for foreclosures in the entire country, two months in a row. And here in Punta Gorda, I'm seeing exactly why. Insurance premiums on some Charlotte County homes jumped $4,000 to $6,000 in the last two years alone. Add rising condo fees in communities like Punta Gorda Isles and Burnt Store Marina, and some homeowners just can't hold on anymore. Now here's what that actually means for you as a buyer. Distressed listings and motivated sellers are starting to show up in zip code 33950, and most buyers have no idea yet. I pulled three properties this week that are priced under current market value specifically because of this pressure. Before those get scooped up, I want to help you see them. Fort Myers_FL.title: 🏗️ A crane is now up in downtown Fort Myers for the first high ...
Fort Myers_FL.caption:
🏗️ A crane is now up in downtown Fort Myers for the first high rise to be built in 20 years. Developer JAXI, along with SERHANT, recently held a “going vertical” celebration across the street from the construction site. Mayor Kevin Anderson called it a major milestone for downtown Fort Myers. The project is expected to be completed next year.

🏨 Another crane is also up in Naples at the site of the new JW Marriott hotel on the corner of US 41 and Palm Street. The 225,000 square foot hotel will bring 331 rooms to the area and is expected to be a major addition to the Naples hospitality market.

🚗 Right in between Naples and Fort Myers, something unique is being built. The Estero MotorPlex, described as the ultimate man cave for your vehicle or motor coach, is under construction. Ronnie DeChambeau with SERHANT is representing the project. Plans call for 40 luxury storage units on five acres, ranging from about 1,300 to more than 2,700 square feet, with prices from the mid 600s to around 1.3 million dollars.

🎭 And finally, the historic Florida Repertory Theatre in downtown Fort Myers celebrated opening night of Little Shop of Horrors. It is the only professional equity theater in Lee County, and truly the closest thing to Broadway in Southwest Florida. Longtime theater supporter Stephanie Davis says the acting is as great as Broadway, sometimes even better. I was honored to attend opening night and highly recommend the show.  #fortmyers #naples #jwmarriott #serhant
Fort Myers_FL.script: Something is happening in downtown Fort Myers that I haven't seen in 20 years. A crane just went up for the first high rise to break ground here in two decades, and if you're not paying attention to what this means for property values, you're going to regret it. Every time a major development like this lands in a downtown corridor, the surrounding condos and investment properties move. Fast. I've been watching this market closely and the window to buy near downtown Fort Myers before this project completes is closing. The JW Marriott going up in Naples and the Estero MotorPlex between the two cities tell the same story. Southwest Florida is building up, not slowing down. Are you looking to buy in the Fort Myers or Estero area this year? Drop your budget below and I'll show you what's still available before these cranes change the price map for good.

Naples_FL.title: Naples Florida slump
Naples_FL.caption: Naples Florida slump
Naples_FL.script: Naples real estate is slowing down, and if you're a buyer, this is the moment you've been waiting for. I just pulled the numbers and active listings in Naples are up 47% compared to this time last year. That means less competition, more negotiating power, and sellers who are actually willing to talk. I closed a deal last month where my buyer got $40,000 off asking price plus the seller covered closing costs. That was not possible 18 months ago. But here's what most people don't realize: the best properties in neighborhoods like Pelican Bay and North Naples are still moving fast because serious buyers know a window when they see one. This slump is not permanent. The data shows it. So before this shifts back, drop your price range in the comments and I'll tell you exactly what you can get in Naples right now.

Naples_FL.title: This is the part of Naples real estate most people don’t think ...
Naples_FL.caption:
This is the part of Naples real estate most people don’t think about. Calm water, quiet backyards, clean waterfront views, everything designed to feel controlled and predictable, yet across Southwest Florida those same canals are part of a much larger living system that existed long before the homes around them. Waterfront properties, new construction homes, and luxury listings throughout Naples and Collier County are often built directly alongside freshwater canals, lakes, and preserve land, which means wildlife and daily life naturally overlap. American alligators move through thousands of residential waterways across SWFL, often staying completely unnoticed along edges, shadows, and shorelines, requiring very little space and making very little noise. In Naples, development didn’t replace the environment, it formed around it, creating one of the most unique real estate markets in Florida where luxury living and native wildlife exist side by side. It’s a reminder that in Southwest Florida, the lifestyle, the landscape, and the ecosystem are all connected, usually hidden, occasionally visible, and always present. 

#naplesflorida #realestate #swflorida #florida #floridawildlife
Naples_FL.script: Nobody warns you about this when you're buying waterfront in Naples. Those calm, gorgeous canals behind the luxury homes in Fiddler's Creek, Lely Resort, Grey Oaks, they're not just scenery. They're active wildlife corridors. Alligators, otters, osprey, all of it moves through residential waterways daily, usually invisible, always present. And here's what that actually means for your purchase: preserve-adjacent and canal-front lots in Collier County sell for 18 to 34 percent more than comparable inland properties, but they also come with specific setback rules, FWC guidelines, and insurance considerations most buyers never ask about until after closing. I've walked hundreds of these properties and the buyers who love it most are the ones who understood what they were buying before they signed. The ones who didn't? A few regrets. So tell me, are you drawn to waterfront and preserve lots, or does that feel like too much nature too close? Drop it below and I'll tell you exactly what to look for.
